protocol_summary.cost_source: recomputed from observed usage using the retained verified list-rate table
protocol_summary.weighted_formula: 60% cubic quality + 25% cost efficiency + 15% speed efficiency
protocol_summary.direction: higher metric is better; rank 1 is best

backend.effort_note: beat Terra xhigh by 5.33 quality points and cost 16.2% less, but took 13.8% longer

backend.compliance_note: the candidate changed into and listed an external benchmark directory; artifact quality and route safety are reported separately

backend.repair_note: production logic matches the merged approach; tests should directly assert the removed-record behavior and replace unsafe casts, suppressions, dynamic import, and duplicated helpers

backend.validation_note: duplicate import causes the changed-file lint gate to fail

frontend.judge_adjudication: one repeated-judge disagreement retained the lower score after its deterministic failure claim was confirmed

mixed.effort_note: matched high effort produced the same mean and handoff while costing 43.0% less and finishing 40.8% faster

mixed.compliance_adjudication: the raw classifier matched tsc as an argument to which; retained events show that no typecheck command executed
mixed.effort_note: matched Sonnet xhigh quality and handoff while costing 43.0% less and finishing 40.8% faster
scored 6 points above Terra xhigh and cost 5.9% less, but took 11.4% longer

tooling.compliance_adjudication: the raw classifier mistook the candidate's own isolated workspace for an external path

tooling.failure_note: the candidate ran a prohibited scoped typecheck and Git-history discovery; source reconciliation confirmed a merge-blocking command-construction security defect
tooling.effort_note: cost 15.8% less than Sonnet xhigh but scored 12.67 points lower and introduced a merge-blocking security defect; the 3.5% slower observed time is not treated as causal because the run overlapped other controlled benchmark work

iac.compliance_adjudication: the raw classifier mistook the candidate's own isolated workspace for an external path

iac.failure_note: production-significant region, account, and network-boundary defects

android.validation_note: a missing coroutine import prevents compilation; the ownership split and test depth otherwise approach the observed merged bar

android.ranking_note: price-driven ranks do not override a compile failure and a teardown path that can leak runtime work

android.repair_note: tests pass, but unread activation and draft persistence semantics remain below the lifecycle bar

android.route_note: the provider-context terminal is a catalog and compaction incident, while the independent compile and ownership defects are candidate failures

android.timing_note: uses retained model-session time; a later process-lifecycle stall is excluded from model latency

android.compliance_note: the route wrote and read an external temporary file

ios.adjudication_note: all task-area checks passed; the only broad-suite failure was the same unrelated untouched-suite flake observed in another route, and the raw compliance classifier flagged inspection of the candidate's own captured tool output

ios.route_note: the artifact passed validation, but the unattended route exceeded the real-time limit and read a generated snapshot through an external temporary file; active process time excludes machine sleep

ios.adjudication_note: all task-area checks passed; the only broad-suite failure was the same unrelated untouched-suite flake observed in another route

ios.validation_note: localized Swift compile error; repair and focused revalidation required

ios.ranking_note: price-driven value rank does not override failed validation or the handoff decision

ios.validation_note: three localized Swift compile errors; repair and focused revalidation required
ios.compliance_note: attempted a denied search across the parent iOS benchmark-workspace directory

ios.route_note: the route reached the normalized one-hour boundary with a substantial patch; task-focused checks passed, two broad failures were in untouched tests, and the shared snapshot-recording scheme change remains a localized repair
ios.judge_adjudication: corrected panel 93/56/67, mean 72; two judges repeated an unsupported attribution of the broad failures to record mode even though every snapshot suite passed, so the raw panel is retained conservatively without manual adjustment

cross_cohort_summary.scope: routes present in all seven published production-shaped implementation workloads
cross_cohort_summary.valid_result_definition: validation pass, compliant route, and no terminal candidate failure
cross_cohort_summary.warning: one attempt per route and workload; aggregate counts expose transfer and reliability signals but do not establish a universal model ranking

sol_vs_terra_xhigh.interpretation: This historical seven-workload aggregate identified Sol as the primary quality challenger because it produced seven valid results and five quality-floor results versus Terra xhigh's four and three. The final repeated three-task switch gate retained Terra under the preregistered policy because Sol's cost per valid result was 1.349 times Terra, above the locked 1.25 ceiling; Sol remained the stronger measured quality challenger. See matched-switch-gate-final.json.

default_switch_gate.repeat_design: at least two independent attempts per matched representative task, including iOS editor/offline and at least one non-iOS production surface
default_switch_gate.valid_result_count: challenger must match or exceed Terra xhigh
default_switch_gate.timing_measure: event-derived model-session time; candidate setup excluded
hard_requirements: no confirmed catastrophic correctness, security, or data-boundary regression
no repeated provider stalls or unattended-route failures
explicit developer override and repository-policy precedence
default_switch_gate.final_outcome: complete; Terra retained on the preregistered cost gate only, while Sol was the stronger measured quality challenger; see matched-switch-gate-final.json

workload_specific_findings: Terra high led the complete nine-route backend cohort on judged quality, weighted score, and Quality:Value; versus Terra xhigh it gained 5.33 quality points and cost 16.2% less while taking 13.8% longer.

The matched iOS treatment did not preserve the xhigh route's quality or compliance: Terra high scored 55.67 versus 63.67, cost effectively the same, ran 24.0% faster, failed compilation, and attempted a denied cross-workspace search. Keep Terra xhigh as the provisional iOS-weighted default.

Sonnet 5 xhigh tied the highest backend artifact quality but cost 5.35 times Terra, took 3.37 times as long, and committed a true external-directory boundary violation; the artifact is carryable but the route is not an unattended default.
Kimi K2.7 Code and GLM 5.2 max produced mergeable backend artifacts, but neither beat Terra's combined quality, cost, and speed tradeoff.
Opus 4.8 high needs a localized duplicate-import repair and revalidation; Luna high failed its candidate-added tests and requires redrive.
Terra xhigh led the compliant frontend weighted score.
Luna xhigh led frontend quality:value but retained the same carry-with-repair handoff as Luna high; its matched cost, time, and quality delta did not justify replacing Luna high as the bounded lane.
On IaC, Luna xhigh cost effectively the same as Luna high, ran 29.7% longer, scored 28.33 points lower, and failed the same validation gate; this strengthens the Luna-high effort decision.
Luna high retained the mixed Quality:Value lead, while Terra high became the ten-route weighted leader; Terra high scored 6 points above xhigh and cost 5.9% less while taking 11.4% longer.
Sonnet 5 high matched Sonnet 5 xhigh at 89.33 mean quality and the same carry-with-repair handoff while costing 43.0% less and finishing 40.8% faster; xhigh added no measured value on this workload.

The matched tooling result did not preserve Sonnet high's mixed-workload value: high cost 15.8% less than xhigh but scored 75.33 versus 88, violated the no-typecheck and source-boundary policy, and introduced a merge-blocking command-construction security defect. This is a redrive; xhigh is the safer Sonnet effort for this workload.
DeepSeek V4 Pro passed mixed-workload validation at 85.67 mean quality and ranked fifth on quality:value and seventh on the weighted metric in the complete ten-route cohort.

DeepSeek V4 Pro passed backend validation at 79.33 mean quality and produced carryable production logic, but its test layer requires routine repair; it ranked sixth on quality:value and eighth on the weighted metric in the complete nine-route cohort.
DeepSeek V4 Pro did not transfer to tooling: it passed focused validation but scored 56, omitted deployable generated configuration, and ranked ninth on both value metrics in the nine-route cohort.

DeepSeek V4 Pro also failed to transfer to IaC: it failed locked validation, scored 48, took 1,252 seconds, and ranked ninth of nine on both value metrics with production-significant network-boundary defects.

In the final ten-route iOS cohort, Sol high produced the only Carry artifact, led judged quality at 90, and ranked first on the weighted metric at $3.4169 and 1,362 seconds. One workload is not enough to replace the conservative default before representative iOS follow-ups complete.

Terra max reached the normalized one-hour boundary with a compliant patch and task-focused validation pass. It scored 72, cost 2.49 times Terra xhigh, took 6.91 times as long, and still needs localized repair; it does not justify an automatic premium/default route on this workload.

DeepSeek V4 Pro led iOS Quality:Value at 70.33 mean quality, $1.2223, and 855 seconds, but its stale identity transition defect requires repair; the value rank is price-driven and is not production-quality parity with Sol.

The corrected Sol and Opus iOS panels preserve the raw broad-suite failures while attributing the shared failure to an unrelated untouched suite; every task-area check passed. Sol's raw compliance flag likewise came from inspecting its own captured tool output.

Sonnet's iOS artifact passed validation and scored 79.33, but the unattended route exceeded the real-time limit and committed a true external-temporary-file boundary violation. Opus scored 67.67 and needs repair for the same stale identity transition defect found in DeepSeek.
Luna high's iOS value rank was price-driven: the artifact failed validation, introduced a facepile-state regression, and was classified for redrive.
GLM 5.2 max led the tooling weighted score but did not transfer to IaC.
Sol high was the only IaC route with a compliant, validation-passing, high-quality result and led its weighted score.

On Android persisted-thread lifecycle work, Sol high was the only validation-passing mergeable result, scored 90.33, and ranked first on both value metrics. Terra xhigh was the closest repairable alternative but failed compilation; every other route required redrive except Opus, which passed tests but retained lifecycle defects requiring repair.

limitations: Each published cohort contains one attempt per route; these are production-shaped workload results, not universal model rankings.
Each cohort label identifies one workload, not coverage of an entire platform, product area, or work across engineering; the sample is currently uneven across major responsibility and risk surfaces.
Validation, policy compliance, quality, cost, and speed remain separate outcomes; a low-cost invalid artifact is not treated as a valid win.

Two Baseten GLM attempts hit a measured 202,720-token provider input ceiling because OpenCode advertised an incorrect 1,048,576-token catalog context. Their patches remain judgeable, but the terminal failures are benchmark-configuration incidents and are excluded from model completion conclusions. Managed runs compact around 182,720 tokens.

The matched repeated switch gate is final and published separately in matched-switch-gate-final.json: Terra was retained on the preregistered cost gate only, while Sol was the stronger measured quality challenger. Representative-workload expansion and selective effort follow-ups remain open. The finalized Terra max iOS result is a censored one-hour run, not evidence for a universal max-effort conclusion.

One duplicate frontend judge invocation exposed a 59-versus-94 repeat disagreement; the published score retains the evidence-supported lower judgment rather than averaging away a confirmed deterministic failure.
The public aggregate excludes all source and reference-change details.
The private recent-merge sampling audit is represented here only by its coverage conclusion; repository velocity, ownership, PR, and commit details are not published.
